documentation:
# Retell Python API library

[![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/retell-sdk.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/retell-sdk/)

The Retell Python library provides convenient access to the Retell REST API from any Python 3.8+
application. The library includes type definitions for all request params and response fields,
and offers both synchronous and asynchronous clients powered by [httpx](https://github.com/encode/httpx).

## Documentation

The REST API documentation can be found on [docs.retellai.com](https://docs.retellai.com/). The full API of this library can be found in [api.md](https://github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/tree/main/api.md).

## Installation

```sh
# install from PyPI
pip install retell-sdk
```

## Usage

The full API of this library can be found in [api.md](https://github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/tree/main/api.md).

```python
from retell import Retell

client = Retell(
    api_key="YOUR_RETELL_API_KEY",
)

agent_response = client.agent.create(
    response_engine={
        "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
        "type": "retell-llm",
    },
    voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
)
print(agent_response.agent_id)
```

## Async usage

Simply import `AsyncRetell` instead of `Retell` and use `await` with each API call:

```python
import asyncio
from retell import AsyncRetell

client = AsyncRetell(
    api_key="YOUR_RETELL_API_KEY",
)


async def main() -> None:
    agent_response = await client.agent.create(
        response_engine={
            "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
            "type": "retell-llm",
        },
        voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
    )
    print(agent_response.agent_id)


asyncio.run(main())
```

Functionality between the synchronous and asynchronous clients is otherwise identical.

## Using types

Nested request parameters are [TypedDicts](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.TypedDict). Responses are [Pydantic models](https://docs.pydantic.dev) which also provide helper methods for things like:

- Serializing back into JSON, `model.to_json()`
- Converting to a dictionary, `model.to_dict()`

Typed requests and responses provide autocomplete and documentation within your editor. If you would like to see type errors in VS Code to help catch bugs earlier, set `python.analysis.typeCheckingMode` to `basic`.

## Handling errors

When the library is unable to connect to the API (for example, due to network connection problems or a timeout), a subclass of `retell.APIConnectionError` is raised.

When the API returns a non-success status code (that is, 4xx or 5xx
response), a subclass of `retell.APIStatusError` is raised, containing `status_code` and `response` properties.

All errors inherit from `retell.APIError`.

```python
import retell
from retell import Retell

client = Retell()

try:
    client.agent.create(
        response_engine={
            "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
            "type": "retell-llm",
        },
        voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
    )
except retell.APIConnectionError as e:
    print("The server could not be reached")
    print(e.__cause__)  # an underlying Exception, likely raised within httpx.
except retell.RateLimitError as e:
    print("A 429 status code was received; we should back off a bit.")
except retell.APIStatusError as e:
    print("Another non-200-range status code was received")
    print(e.status_code)
    print(e.response)
```

Error codes are as followed:

| Status Code | Error Type                 |
| ----------- | -------------------------- |
| 400         | `BadRequestError`          |
| 401         | `AuthenticationError`      |
| 403         | `PermissionDeniedError`    |
| 404         | `NotFoundError`            |
| 422         | `UnprocessableEntityError` |
| 429         | `RateLimitError`           |
| >=500       | `InternalServerError`      |
| N/A         | `APIConnectionError`       |

### Retries

Certain errors are automatically retried 2 times by default, with a short exponential backoff.
Connection errors (for example, due to a network connectivity problem), 408 Request Timeout, 409 Conflict,
429 Rate Limit, and >=500 Internal errors are all retried by default.

You can use the `max_retries` option to configure or disable retry settings:

```python
from retell import Retell

# Configure the default for all requests:
client = Retell(
    # default is 2
    max_retries=0,
)

# Or, configure per-request:
client.with_options(max_retries=5).agent.create(
    response_engine={
        "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
        "type": "retell-llm",
    },
    voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
)
```

### Timeouts

By default requests time out after 1 minute. You can configure this with a `timeout` option,
which accepts a float or an [`httpx.Timeout`](https://www.python-httpx.org/advanced/#fine-tuning-the-configuration) object:

```python
from retell import Retell

# Configure the default for all requests:
client = Retell(
    # 20 seconds (default is 1 minute)
    timeout=20.0,
)

# More granular control:
client = Retell(
    timeout=httpx.Timeout(60.0, read=5.0, write=10.0, connect=2.0),
)

# Override per-request:
client.with_options(timeout=5.0).agent.create(
    response_engine={
        "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
        "type": "retell-llm",
    },
    voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
)
```

On timeout, an `APITimeoutError` is thrown.

Note that requests that time out are [retried twice by default](https://github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/tree/main/#retries).

## Advanced

### Logging

We use the standard library [`logging`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html) module.

You can enable logging by setting the environment variable `RETELL_LOG` to `info`.

```shell
$ export RETELL_LOG=info
```

Or to `debug` for more verbose logging.

### How to tell whether `None` means `null` or missing

In an API response, a field may be explicitly `null`, or missing entirely; in either case, its value is `None` in this library. You can differentiate the two cases with `.model_fields_set`:

```py
if response.my_field is None:
  if 'my_field' not in response.model_fields_set:
    print('Got json like {}, without a "my_field" key present at all.')
  else:
    print('Got json like {"my_field": null}.')
```

### Accessing raw response data (e.g. headers)

The "raw" Response object can be accessed by prefixing `.with_raw_response.` to any HTTP method call, e.g.,

```py
from retell import Retell

client = Retell()
response = client.agent.with_raw_response.create(
    response_engine={
        "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
        "type": "retell-llm",
    },
    voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
)
print(response.headers.get('X-My-Header'))

agent = response.parse()  # get the object that `agent.create()` would have returned
print(agent.agent_id)
```

These methods return an [`APIResponse`](https://github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/tree/main/src/retell/_response.py) object.

The async client returns an [`AsyncAPIResponse`](https://github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/tree/main/src/retell/_response.py) with the same structure, the only difference being `await`able methods for reading the response content.

#### `.with_streaming_response`

The above interface eagerly reads the full response body when you make the request, which may not always be what you want.

To stream the response body, use `.with_streaming_response` instead, which requires a context manager and only reads the response body once you call `.read()`, `.text()`, `.json()`, `.iter_bytes()`, `.iter_text()`, `.iter_lines()` or `.parse()`. In the async client, these are async methods.

```python
with client.agent.with_streaming_response.create(
    response_engine={
        "llm_id": "llm_234sdertfsdsfsdf",
        "type": "retell-llm",
    },
    voice_id="11labs-Adrian",
) as response:
    print(response.headers.get("X-My-Header"))

    for line in response.iter_lines():
        print(line)
```

The context manager is required so that the response will reliably be closed.

### Making custom/undocumented requests

This library is typed for convenient access to the documented API.

If you need to access undocumented endpoints, params, or response properties, the library can still be used.

#### Undocumented endpoints

To make requests to undocumented endpoints, you can make requests using `client.get`, `client.post`, and other
http verbs. Options on the client will be respected (such as retries) will be respected when making this
request.

```py
import httpx

response = client.post(
    "/foo",
    cast_to=httpx.Response,
    body={"my_param": True},
)

print(response.headers.get("x-foo"))
```

#### Undocumented request params

If you want to explicitly send an extra param, you can do so with the `extra_query`, `extra_body`, and `extra_headers` request
options.

#### Undocumented response properties

To access undocumented response properties, you can access the extra fields like `response.unknown_prop`. You
can also get all the extra fields on the Pydantic model as a dict with
[`response.model_extra`](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/api/base_model/#pydantic.BaseModel.model_extra).

### Configuring the HTTP client

You can directly override the [httpx client](https://www.python-httpx.org/api/#client) to customize it for your use case, including:

- Support for proxies
- Custom transports
- Additional [advanced](https://www.python-httpx.org/advanced/clients/) functionality

```python
from retell import Retell, DefaultHttpxClient

client = Retell(
    # Or use the `RETELL_BASE_URL` env var
    base_url="http://my.test.server.example.com:8083",
    http_client=DefaultHttpxClient(
        proxies="http://my.test.proxy.example.com",
        transport=httpx.HTTPTransport(local_address="0.0.0.0"),
    ),
)
```

You can also customize the client on a per-request basis by using `with_options()`:

```python
client.with_options(http_client=DefaultHttpxClient(...))
```

### Managing HTTP resources

By default the library closes underlying HTTP connections whenever the client is [garbage collected](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__del__). You can manually close the client using the `.close()` method if desired, or with a context manager that closes when exiting.

## Versioning

This package generally follows [SemVer](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html) conventions, though certain backwards-incompatible changes may be released as minor versions:

1. Changes that only affect static types, without breaking runtime behavior.
2. Changes to library internals which are technically public but not intended or documented for external use. _(Please open a GitHub issue to let us know if you are relying on such internals)_.
3. Changes that we do not expect to impact the vast majority of users in practice.

We take backwards-compatibility seriously and work hard to ensure you can rely on a smooth upgrade experience.

We are keen for your feedback; please open an [issue](https://www.github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/issues) with questions, bugs, or suggestions.

### Determining the installed version

If you've upgraded to the latest version but aren't seeing any new features you were expecting then your python environment is likely still using an older version.

You can determine the version that is being used at runtime with:

```py
import retell
print(retell.__version__)
```

## Requirements

Python 3.8 or higher.

## Contributing

See [the contributing documentation](https://github.com/RetellAI/retell-python-sdk/tree/main/./CONTRIBUTING.md).

functions.name: retell._utils._typing.extract_type_var_from_base
documentation:
Given a type like `Foo[T]`, returns the generic type variable `T`.

This also handles the case where a concrete subclass is given, e.g.
```py
class MyResponse(Foo[bytes]):
    ...

extract_type_var(MyResponse, bases=(Foo,), index=0) -> bytes
```

And where a generic subclass is given:
```py
_T = TypeVar('_T')
class MyResponse(Foo[_T]):
    ...

extract_type_var(MyResponse[bytes], bases=(Foo,), index=0) -> bytes
```

functions.name: retell._utils._reflection.function_has_argument
documentation: Returns whether or not the given function has a specific parameter

functions.name: retell._utils._reflection.assert_signatures_in_sync
documentation: Ensure that the signature of the second function matches the first.

functions.name: retell._utils._sync.to_thread
documentation:
Asynchronously run function *func* in a separate thread.

Any *args and \*\*kwargs supplied for this function are directly passed
to \*func*. Also, the current {class}`contextvars.Context` is propagated,
allowing context variables from the main thread to be accessed in the
separate thread.

Returns a coroutine that can be awaited to get the eventual result of *func*.
backport of https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-task.html#asyncio.to_thread
for Python 3.8 support

functions.name: retell._utils._sync.asyncify
documentation:
Take a blocking function and create an async one that receives the same
positional and keyword arguments. For python version 3.9 and above, it uses
asyncio.to_thread to run the function in a separate thread. For python version
3.8, it uses locally defined copy of the asyncio.to_thread function which was
introduced in python 3.9.

Usage:

```python
def blocking_func(arg1, arg2, kwarg1=None):
    # blocking code
    return result


result = asyncify(blocking_function)(arg1, arg2, kwarg1=value1)
```

## Arguments

`function`: a blocking regular callable (e.g. a function)

## Return

An async function that takes the same positional and keyword arguments as the
original one, that when called runs the same original function in a thread worker
and returns the result.
inspired by `asyncer`, https://github.com/tiangolo/asyncer

functions.name: retell._utils._utils.extract_files

Recursively extract files from the given dictionary based on specified paths.

A path may look like this ['foo', 'files', '<array>', 'data'].

Note: this mutates the given dictionary.

functions.name: retell._utils._utils.is_tuple
documentation: Type safe methods for narrowing types with TypeVars.
The default narrowing for isinstance(obj, dict) is dict[unknown, unknown],
however this cause Pyright to rightfully report errors. As we know we don't
care about the contained types we can safely use `object` in it's place.
There are two separate functions defined, `is_*` and `is_*_t` for different use cases.
`is_*` is for when you're dealing with an unknown input
`is_*_t` is for when you're narrowing a known union type to a specific subset

functions.name: retell._utils._utils.deepcopy_minimal
documentation:
Minimal reimplementation of copy.deepcopy() that will only copy certain object types:

- mappings, e.g. `dict`
- list

This is done for performance reasons.

functions.name: retell._utils._utils.quote

functions.name: retell._utils._utils.required_args
documentation:
Decorator to enforce a given set of arguments or variants of arguments are passed to the decorated function.

Useful for enforcing runtime validation of overloaded functions.

Example usage:
```py
@overload
def foo(*, a: str) -> str: ...


@overload
def foo(*, b: bool) -> str: ...


# This enforces the same constraints that a static type checker would
# i.e. that either a or b must be passed to the function
@required_args(["a"], ["b"])
def foo(*, a: str | None = None, b: bool | None = None) -> str: ...
```

documentation:
Wrapper over `transform()` that allows `None` to be passed.

See `transform()` for more details.

functions.name: retell._utils._transform.transform
documentation:
Transform dictionaries based off of type information from the given type, for example:

```py
class Params(TypedDict, total=False):
    card_id: Required[Annotated[str, PropertyInfo(alias="cardID")]]


transformed = transform({"card_id": "<my card ID>"}, Params)
# {'cardID': '<my card ID>'}
```

Any keys / data that does not have type information given will be included as is.

It should be noted that the transformations that this function does are not represented in the type system.
Wrapper over _transform_recursive providing fake types

functions.name: retell._utils._transform._maybe_transform_key

Note: this function only looks at `Annotated` types that contain `PropertInfo` metadata.

functions.name: retell._utils._transform._transform_recursive
documentation:
Transform the given data against the expected type.

Args:
    annotation: The direct type annotation given to the particular piece of data.
        This may or may not be wrapped in metadata types, e.g. `Required[T]`, `Annotated[T, ...]` etc

    inner_type: If applicable, this is the "inside" type. This is useful in certain cases where the outside type
        is a container type such as `List[T]`. In that case `inner_type` should be set to `T` so that each entry in
        the list can be transformed using the metadata from the container type.

        Defaults to the same value as the `annotation` argument.

classes.name: retell._utils._transform.PropertyInfo
documentation:
Metadata class to be used in Annotated types to provide information about a given type.

For example:

class MyParams(TypedDict):
    account_holder_name: Annotated[str, PropertyInfo(alias='accountHolderName')]

This means that {'account_holder_name': 'Robert'} will be transformed to {'accountHolderName': 'Robert'} before being sent to the API.

used as pagination key here. If not set, will start from the beginning.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.agent_name
documentation: The name of the agent. Only used for your own reference.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.ambient_sound
documentation:
If set, will add ambient environment sound to the call to make experience more
realistic. Currently supports the following options:

- `coffee-shop`: Coffee shop ambience with people chatting in background.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/coffee-shop.wav)

- `convention-hall`: Convention hall ambience, with some echo and people
  chatting in background.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/convention-hall.wav)

- `summer-outdoor`: Summer outdoor ambience with cicada chirping.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/summer-outdoor.wav)

- `mountain-outdoor`: Mountain outdoor ambience with birds singing.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/mountain-outdoor.wav)

- `static-noise`: Constant static noise.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/static-noise.wav)

- `call-center`: Call center work noise.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/call-center.wav)

Set to `null` to remove ambient sound from this agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.ambient_sound_volume
documentation:
If set, will control the volume of the ambient sound.

Value ranging from [0,2]. Lower value means quieter ambient sound, while higher
value means louder ambient sound. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.backchannel_frequency
documentation:
Only applicable when enable_backchannel is true.

Controls how often the agent would backchannel when a backchannel is possible.
Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means less frequent backchannel, while
higher value means more frequent backchannel. If unset, default value 0.8 will
apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.backchannel_words
documentation:
Only applicable when enable_backchannel is true.

A list of words that the agent would use as backchannel. If not set, default
backchannel words will apply. Check out
[backchannel default words](/agent/interaction-configuration#backchannel) for
more details. Note that certain voices do not work too well with certain words,
so it's recommended to expeirment before adding any words.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.boosted_keywords
documentation:
Provide a customized list of keywords to bias the transcriber model, so that
these words are more likely to get transcribed. Commonly used for names, brands,
street, etc.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.enable_backchannel
documentation:
Controls whether the agent would backchannel (agent interjects the speaker with
phrases like "yeah", "uh-huh" to signify interest and engagement). Backchannel
when enabled tends to show up more in longer user utterances. If not set, agent
will not backchannel.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.enable_transcription_formatting
documentation:
If set to true, will format transcription to number, date, email, etc.

If set to false, will return transcripts in raw words. If not set, default value
of true will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.enable_voicemail_detection
documentation:
If set to true, will detect whether the call enters a voicemail.

Note that this feature is only available for phone calls.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.end_call_after_silence_ms
documentation:
If users stay silent for a period after agent speech, end the call.

The minimum value allowed is 10,000 ms (10 s). By default, this is set to 600000
(10 min).

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.fallback_voice_ids
documentation:
When TTS provider for the selected voice is experiencing outages, we would use
fallback voices listed here for the agent. Voice id and the fallback voice ids
must be from different TTS providers. The system would go through the list in
order, if the first one in the list is also having outage, it would use the next
one. Set to null to remove voice fallback for the agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.interruption_sensitivity
documentation:
Controls how sensitive the agent is to user interruptions.

Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means it will take longer / more words for
user to interrupt agent, while higher value means it's easier for user to
interrupt agent. If unset, default value 1 will apply. When this is set to 0,
agent would never be interrupted.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.language
documentation:
Specifies what language (and dialect) the speech recognition will operate in.

For instance, selecting `en-GB` optimizes speech recognition for British
English. If unset, will use default value `en-US`. Select `multi` for
multilingual support, currently this supports Spanish and English.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.max_call_duration_ms
documentation:
Maximum allowed length for the call, will force end the call if reached.

The minimum value allowed is 60,000 ms (1 min), and maximum value allowed is
7,200,000 (2 hours). By default, this is set to 3,600,000 (1 hour).

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.normalize_for_speech
documentation:
If set to true, will normalize the some part of text (number, currency, date,
etc) to spoken to its spoken form for more consistent speech synthesis
(sometimes the voice synthesize system itself might read these wrong with the
raw text). For example, it will convert "Call my number 2137112342 on Jul 5th,
2024 for the $24.12 payment" to "Call my number two one three seven one one two
three four two on july fifth, twenty twenty four for the twenty four dollars
twelve cents payment" before starting audio generation.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.opt_out_sensitive_data_storage
documentation:
Whether this agent opts out of sensitive data storage like transcript,
recording, logging. These data can still be accessed securely via webhooks. If
not set, default value of false will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.post_call_analysis_data
documentation:
Post call analysis data to extract from the call.

This data will augment the pre-defined variables extracted in the call analysis.
This will be available after the call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.pronunciation_dictionary
documentation:
A list of words / phrases and their pronunciation to be used to guide the audio
synthesize for consistent pronunciation. Currently only supported for English &
11labs voices. Set to null to remove pronunciation dictionary from this agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.reminder_max_count
documentation:
If set, controls how many times agent would remind user when user is
unresponsive. Must be a non negative integer. If unset, default value of 1 will
apply (remind once). Set to 0 to disable agent from reminding.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.reminder_trigger_ms
documentation:
If set (in milliseconds), will trigger a reminder to the agent to speak if the
user has been silent for the specified duration after some agent speech. Must be
a positive number. If unset, default value of 10000 ms (10 s) will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.response_engine
documentation: The response engine to use for the agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.responsiveness
documentation:
Controls how responsive is the agent.

Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means less responsive agent (wait more,
respond slower), while higher value means faster exchanges (respond when it
can). If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.voice_id
documentation:
Unique voice id used for the agent.

Find list of available voices and their preview in Dashboard.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.voice_model
documentation:
Optionally set the voice model used for the selected voice.

Currently only elevenlab voices have voice model selections. Set to null to
remove voice model selection, and default ones will apply. Supported voice
models are:

- `eleven_turbo_v2`: Fast english only model, supports pronunciation tags.

- `eleven_turbo_v2_5`: Multilingual model with lowest latency.

- `eleven_multilingual_v2`: Multilingual model with rich emotion and nice
  accent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.voice_speed
documentation:
Controls speed of voice.

Value ranging from [0.5,2]. Lower value means slower speech, while higher value
means faster speech rate. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.voice_temperature
documentation:
Controls how stable the voice is.

Value ranging from [0,2]. Lower value means more stable, and higher value means
more variant speech generation. Currently this setting only applies to `11labs`
voices. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.voicemail_detection_timeout_ms
documentation:
Configures when to stop running voicemail detection, as it becomes unlikely to
hit voicemail after a couple minutes, and keep running it will only have
negative impact. The minimum value allowed is 5,000 ms (5 s), and maximum value
allowed is 180,000 (3 minutes). By default, this is set to 30,000 (30 s).

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.voicemail_message
documentation:
The message to be played when the call enters a voicemail.

Note that this feature is only available for phone calls. If you want to hangup
after hitting voicemail, set this to empty string.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.volume
documentation:
If set, will control the volume of the agent.

Value ranging from [0,2]. Lower value means quieter agent speech, while higher
value means louder agent speech. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_update_params.AgentUpdateParams.webhook_url
documentation:
The webhook for agent to listen to call events.

See what events it would get at [webhook doc](/features/webhook). If set, will
binds webhook events for this agent to the specified url, and will ignore the
account level webhook for this agent. Set to `null` to remove webhook url from
this agent.

This needs to be passed to your frontend to join the call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.agent_id

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.call_id
documentation:

Used to identify in LLM websocket and used to authenticate in audio websocket.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.call_status
documentation:
Status of call.

- `registered`: Call id issued, starting to make a call using this id.

- `ongoing`: Call connected and ongoing.

- `ended`: The underlying websocket has ended for the call. Either user or agent
  hanged up, or call transferred.

- `error`: Call encountered error.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.call_type
documentation: Type of the call. Used to distinguish between web call and phone call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.call_analysis
documentation:
Post call analysis that includes information such as sentiment, status, summary,
and custom defined data to extract. Available after call ends. Subscribe to
`call_analyzed` webhook event type to receive it once ready.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.disconnection_reason
documentation:
The reason for the disconnection of the call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.e2e_latency
documentation:
End to end latency (from user stops talking to agent start talking) tracking of
the call, available after call ends. This latency does not account for the
network trip time from Retell server to user frontend. The latency is tracked
every time turn change between user and agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.end_timestamp
documentation:
End timestamp (milliseconds since epoch) of the call.

Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.llm_latency
documentation:
LLM latency (from issue of LLM call to first token received) tracking of the
call, available after call ends. When using custom LLM. this latency includes
LLM websocket roundtrip time between user server and Retell server.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.llm_websocket_network_rtt_latency
documentation:
LLM websocket roundtrip latency (between user server and Retell server) tracking
of the call, available after call ends. Only populated for calls using custom
LLM.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.metadata
documentation:
An arbitrary object for storage purpose only.

You can put anything here like your internal customer id associated with the
call. Not used for processing. You can later get this field from the call
object.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.opt_out_sensitive_data_storage
documentation:
Whether this call opts out of sensitive data storage like transcript, recording,
logging.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.public_log_url
documentation:
Public log of the call, containing details about all the requests and responses
received in LLM WebSocket, latency tracking for each turntaking, helpful for
debugging and tracing. Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.recording_url
documentation: Recording of the call. Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.retell_llm_dynamic_variables
documentation:
Add optional dynamic variables in key value pairs of string that injects into
your Retell LLM prompt and tool description. Only applicable for Retell LLM.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.start_timestamp
documentation:
Begin timestamp (milliseconds since epoch) of the call.

Available after call starts.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.transcript
documentation: Transcription of the call. Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.transcript_object
documentation:
Transcript of the call in the format of a list of utterance, with timestamp.

Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.web_call_response.WebCallResponse.transcript_with_tool_calls
documentation:
Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results.

It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked
and what was the result. Available after call ends.

documentation:

Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a
maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.State.edges
documentation: Edges of the state define how and what state can be reached from this state.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.State.state_prompt
documentation:
Prompt of the state, will be appended to the system prompt of LLM.

- System prompt = general prompt + state prompt.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.State.tools
documentation:
A list of tools specific to this state the model may call (to get external
knowledge, call API, etc). You can select from some common predefined tools like
end call, transfer call, etc; or you can create your own custom tool (last
option) for the LLM to use.

- Tools of LLM = general tools + state tools + state transitions

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.last_modification_timestamp
documentation:
Last modification timestamp (milliseconds since epoch).

Either the time of last update or creation if no updates available.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.llm_id
documentation: Unique id of Retell LLM.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.begin_message
documentation:
First utterance said by the agent in the call.

If not set, LLM will dynamically generate a message. If set to "", agent will
wait for user to speak first.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.general_prompt
documentation:
General prompt appended to system prompt no matter what state the agent is in.

- System prompt (with state) = general prompt + state prompt.

- System prompt (no state) = general prompt.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.general_tools
documentation:
A list of tools the model may call (to get external knowledge, call API, etc).

You can select from some common predefined tools like end call, transfer call,
etc; or you can create your own custom tool (last option) for the LLM to use.

- Tools of LLM (with state) = general tools + state tools + state transitions

- Tools of LLM (no state) = general tools

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.inbound_dynamic_variables_webhook_url
documentation:
For inbound phone calls, if this webhook is set, will POST to it to retrieve
dynamic variables to use for the call. Without this, there's no way to pass
dynamic variables for inbound calls.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.knowledge_base_ids
documentation:
A list of knowledge base ids to use for this resource.

Set to null to remove all knowledge bases.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.model
documentation: Select the underlying text LLM. If not set, would default to gpt-4o.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.api_model_temperature
documentation:
If set, will control the randomness of the response.

Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means more deterministic, while higher
value means more random. If unset, default value 0 will apply. Note that for
tool calling, a lower value is recommended.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.s2s_model
documentation:
Select the underlying speech to speech model.

Can only set this or model, not both.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.starting_state
documentation: Name of the starting state. Required if states is not empty.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.states
documentation:
States of the LLM.

This is to help reduce prompt length and tool choices when the call can be
broken into distinct states. With shorter prompts and less tools, the LLM can
better focus and follow the rules, minimizing hallucination. If this field is
not set, the agent would only have general prompt and general tools (essentially
one state).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_response.LlmResponse.tool_call_strict_mode
documentation:
Only applicable when model is gpt-4o or gpt-4o mini.

If set to true, will use structured output to make sure tool call arguments
follow the json schema. The time to save a new tool or change to a tool will be
longer as additional processing is needed. Default to false.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.ambient_sound
documentation:
If set, will add ambient environment sound to the call to make experience more
realistic. Currently supports the following options:

- `coffee-shop`: Coffee shop ambience with people chatting in background.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/coffee-shop.wav)

- `convention-hall`: Convention hall ambience, with some echo and people
  chatting in background.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/convention-hall.wav)

- `summer-outdoor`: Summer outdoor ambience with cicada chirping.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/summer-outdoor.wav)

- `mountain-outdoor`: Mountain outdoor ambience with birds singing.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/mountain-outdoor.wav)

- `static-noise`: Constant static noise.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/static-noise.wav)

- `call-center`: Call center work noise.
  [Listen to Ambience](https://retell-utils-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/call-center.wav)

Set to `null` to remove ambient sound from this agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.ambient_sound_volume
documentation:
If set, will control the volume of the ambient sound.

Value ranging from [0,2]. Lower value means quieter ambient sound, while higher
value means louder ambient sound. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.backchannel_frequency
documentation:
Only applicable when enable_backchannel is true.

Controls how often the agent would backchannel when a backchannel is possible.
Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means less frequent backchannel, while
higher value means more frequent backchannel. If unset, default value 0.8 will
apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.backchannel_words
documentation:
Only applicable when enable_backchannel is true.

A list of words that the agent would use as backchannel. If not set, default
backchannel words will apply. Check out
[backchannel default words](/agent/interaction-configuration#backchannel) for
more details. Note that certain voices do not work too well with certain words,
so it's recommended to expeirment before adding any words.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.boosted_keywords
documentation:
Provide a customized list of keywords to bias the transcriber model, so that
these words are more likely to get transcribed. Commonly used for names, brands,
street, etc.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.enable_backchannel
documentation:
Controls whether the agent would backchannel (agent interjects the speaker with
phrases like "yeah", "uh-huh" to signify interest and engagement). Backchannel
when enabled tends to show up more in longer user utterances. If not set, agent
will not backchannel.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.enable_transcription_formatting
documentation:
If set to true, will format transcription to number, date, email, etc.

If set to false, will return transcripts in raw words. If not set, default value
of true will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.enable_voicemail_detection
documentation:
If set to true, will detect whether the call enters a voicemail.

Note that this feature is only available for phone calls.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.end_call_after_silence_ms
documentation:
If users stay silent for a period after agent speech, end the call.

The minimum value allowed is 10,000 ms (10 s). By default, this is set to 600000
(10 min).

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.fallback_voice_ids
documentation:
When TTS provider for the selected voice is experiencing outages, we would use
fallback voices listed here for the agent. Voice id and the fallback voice ids
must be from different TTS providers. The system would go through the list in
order, if the first one in the list is also having outage, it would use the next
one. Set to null to remove voice fallback for the agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.interruption_sensitivity
documentation:
Controls how sensitive the agent is to user interruptions.

Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means it will take longer / more words for
user to interrupt agent, while higher value means it's easier for user to
interrupt agent. If unset, default value 1 will apply. When this is set to 0,
agent would never be interrupted.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.language
documentation:
Specifies what language (and dialect) the speech recognition will operate in.

For instance, selecting `en-GB` optimizes speech recognition for British
English. If unset, will use default value `en-US`. Select `multi` for
multilingual support, currently this supports Spanish and English.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.max_call_duration_ms
documentation:
Maximum allowed length for the call, will force end the call if reached.

The minimum value allowed is 60,000 ms (1 min), and maximum value allowed is
7,200,000 (2 hours). By default, this is set to 3,600,000 (1 hour).

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.normalize_for_speech
documentation:
If set to true, will normalize the some part of text (number, currency, date,
etc) to spoken to its spoken form for more consistent speech synthesis
(sometimes the voice synthesize system itself might read these wrong with the
raw text). For example, it will convert "Call my number 2137112342 on Jul 5th,
2024 for the $24.12 payment" to "Call my number two one three seven one one two
three four two on july fifth, twenty twenty four for the twenty four dollars
twelve cents payment" before starting audio generation.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.opt_out_sensitive_data_storage
documentation:
Whether this agent opts out of sensitive data storage like transcript,
recording, logging. These data can still be accessed securely via webhooks. If
not set, default value of false will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.post_call_analysis_data
documentation:
Post call analysis data to extract from the call.

This data will augment the pre-defined variables extracted in the call analysis.
This will be available after the call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.pronunciation_dictionary
documentation:
A list of words / phrases and their pronunciation to be used to guide the audio
synthesize for consistent pronunciation. Currently only supported for English &
11labs voices. Set to null to remove pronunciation dictionary from this agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.reminder_max_count
documentation:
If set, controls how many times agent would remind user when user is
unresponsive. Must be a non negative integer. If unset, default value of 1 will
apply (remind once). Set to 0 to disable agent from reminding.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.reminder_trigger_ms
documentation:
If set (in milliseconds), will trigger a reminder to the agent to speak if the
user has been silent for the specified duration after some agent speech. Must be
a positive number. If unset, default value of 10000 ms (10 s) will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.responsiveness
documentation:
Controls how responsive is the agent.

Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means less responsive agent (wait more,
respond slower), while higher value means faster exchanges (respond when it
can). If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.voice_model
documentation:
Optionally set the voice model used for the selected voice.

Currently only elevenlab voices have voice model selections. Set to null to
remove voice model selection, and default ones will apply. Supported voice
models are:

- `eleven_turbo_v2`: Fast english only model, supports pronunciation tags.

- `eleven_turbo_v2_5`: Multilingual model with lowest latency.

- `eleven_multilingual_v2`: Multilingual model with rich emotion and nice
  accent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.voice_speed
documentation:
Controls speed of voice.

Value ranging from [0.5,2]. Lower value means slower speech, while higher value
means faster speech rate. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.voice_temperature
documentation:
Controls how stable the voice is.

Value ranging from [0,2]. Lower value means more stable, and higher value means
more variant speech generation. Currently this setting only applies to `11labs`
voices. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.voicemail_detection_timeout_ms
documentation:
Configures when to stop running voicemail detection, as it becomes unlikely to
hit voicemail after a couple minutes, and keep running it will only have
negative impact. The minimum value allowed is 5,000 ms (5 s), and maximum value
allowed is 180,000 (3 minutes). By default, this is set to 30,000 (30 s).

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.voicemail_message
documentation:
The message to be played when the call enters a voicemail.

Note that this feature is only available for phone calls. If you want to hangup
after hitting voicemail, set this to empty string.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.volume
documentation:
If set, will control the volume of the agent.

Value ranging from [0,2]. Lower value means quieter agent speech, while higher
value means louder agent speech. If unset, default value 1 will apply.

class_variables.name: retell.types.agent_response.AgentResponse.webhook_url
documentation:
The webhook for agent to listen to call events.

See what events it would get at [webhook doc](/features/webhook). If set, will
binds webhook events for this agent to the specified url, and will ignore the
account level webhook for this agent. Set to `null` to remove webhook url from
this agent.

End time of the word in the call in second.

This is relative audio time, not wall time.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptObjectWord.start
documentation:
Start time of the word in the call in second.

This is relative audio time, not wall time.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptObjectWord.word
documentation: Word transcript (with punctuation if applicable).

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptObject.content
documentation: Transcript of the utterances.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptObject.role
documentation: Documents whether this utterance is spoken by agent or user.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptObject.words
documentation:
Array of words in the utterance with the word timestamp.

Useful for understanding what word was spoken at what time. Note that the word
timestamp is not guaranteed to be accurate, it's more like an approximation.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallUtteranceWord.end
documentation:

This is relative audio time, not wall time.

Start time of the word in the call in second.

This is relative audio time, not wall time.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallUtteranceWord.word

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallUtterance.content
documentation: Transcript of the utterances.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallUtterance.role
documentation: Documents whether this utterance is spoken by agent or user.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallToolCallInvocationUtterance.name

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallToolCallInvocationUtterance.role
documentation: This is a tool call invocation.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallToolCallInvocationUtterance.tool_call_id

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallToolCallResultUtterance.content

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallToolCallResultUtterance.role

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.TranscriptWithToolCallToolCallResultUtterance.tool_call_id
documentation: Tool call id, globally unique.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.agent_id
documentation: Corresponding agent id of this call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.call_id
documentation:
Unique id of the call.

Used to identify in LLM websocket and used to authenticate in audio websocket.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.call_status
documentation:
Status of call.

- `registered`: Call id issued, starting to make a call using this id.

- `ongoing`: Call connected and ongoing.

- `ended`: The underlying websocket has ended for the call. Either user or agent
  hanged up, or call transferred.

- `error`: Call encountered error.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.call_type
documentation: Type of the call. Used to distinguish between web call and phone call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.direction
documentation: Direction of the phone call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.from_number
documentation: The caller number.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.to_number
documentation: The callee number.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.call_analysis
documentation:
Post call analysis that includes information such as sentiment, status, summary,
and custom defined data to extract. Available after call ends. Subscribe to
`call_analyzed` webhook event type to receive it once ready.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.disconnection_reason
documentation:
The reason for the disconnection of the call.

Read details desciption about reasons listed here at
[Disconnection Reason Doc](/get-started/debug-guide#disconnection-reason).

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.e2e_latency
documentation:
End to end latency (from user stops talking to agent start talking) tracking of
the call, available after call ends. This latency does not account for the
network trip time from Retell server to user frontend. The latency is tracked
every time turn change between user and agent.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.end_timestamp
documentation:
End timestamp (milliseconds since epoch) of the call.

Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.llm_latency
documentation:
LLM latency (from issue of LLM call to first token received) tracking of the
call, available after call ends. When using custom LLM. this latency includes
LLM websocket roundtrip time between user server and Retell server.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.llm_websocket_network_rtt_latency
documentation:
LLM websocket roundtrip latency (between user server and Retell server) tracking
of the call, available after call ends. Only populated for calls using custom
LLM.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.metadata
documentation:
An arbitrary object for storage purpose only.

You can put anything here like your internal customer id associated with the
call. Not used for processing. You can later get this field from the call
object.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.opt_out_sensitive_data_storage
documentation:
Whether this call opts out of sensitive data storage like transcript, recording,
logging.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.public_log_url
documentation:
Public log of the call, containing details about all the requests and responses
received in LLM WebSocket, latency tracking for each turntaking, helpful for
debugging and tracing. Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.recording_url
documentation: Recording of the call. Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.retell_llm_dynamic_variables
documentation:
Add optional dynamic variables in key value pairs of string that injects into
your Retell LLM prompt and tool description. Only applicable for Retell LLM.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.start_timestamp
documentation:
Begin timestamp (milliseconds since epoch) of the call.

Available after call starts.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.transcript
documentation: Transcription of the call. Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.transcript_object
documentation:
Transcript of the call in the format of a list of utterance, with timestamp.

Available after call ends.

class_variables.name: retell.types.phone_call_response.PhoneCallResponse.transcript_with_tool_calls
documentation:
Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results.

It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked
and what was the result. Available after call ends.

modules.name: retell.types.llm_update_params
documentation: File generated from our OpenAPI spec by Stainless. See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.begin_message
documentation:
First utterance said by the agent in the call.

If not set, LLM will dynamically generate a message. If set to "", agent will
wait for user to speak first.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.general_prompt
documentation:
General prompt appended to system prompt no matter what state the agent is in.

- System prompt (with state) = general prompt + state prompt.

- System prompt (no state) = general prompt.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.general_tools
documentation:
A list of tools the model may call (to get external knowledge, call API, etc).

You can select from some common predefined tools like end call, transfer call,
etc; or you can create your own custom tool (last option) for the LLM to use.

- Tools of LLM (with state) = general tools + state tools + state transitions

- Tools of LLM (no state) = general tools

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.inbound_dynamic_variables_webhook_url
documentation:
For inbound phone calls, if this webhook is set, will POST to it to retrieve
dynamic variables to use for the call. Without this, there's no way to pass
dynamic variables for inbound calls.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.knowledge_base_ids
documentation:
A list of knowledge base ids to use for this resource.

Set to null to remove all knowledge bases.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.model
documentation: Select the underlying text LLM. If not set, would default to gpt-4o.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.model_temperature
documentation:
If set, will control the randomness of the response.

Value ranging from [0,1]. Lower value means more deterministic, while higher
value means more random. If unset, default value 0 will apply. Note that for
tool calling, a lower value is recommended.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.s2s_model
documentation:
Select the underlying speech to speech model.

Can only set this or model, not both.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.starting_state
documentation: Name of the starting state. Required if states is not empty.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.states
documentation:
States of the LLM.

This is to help reduce prompt length and tool choices when the call can be
broken into distinct states. With shorter prompts and less tools, the LLM can
better focus and follow the rules, minimizing hallucination. If this field is
not set, the agent would only have general prompt and general tools (essentially
one state).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.LlmUpdateParams.tool_call_strict_mode
documentation:
Only applicable when model is gpt-4o or gpt-4o mini.

If set to true, will use structured output to make sure tool call arguments
follow the json schema. The time to save a new tool or change to a tool will be
longer as additional processing is needed. Default to false.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolEndCallTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state transitions). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolEndCallTool.description
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallToolWarmTransferOptionWarmTransferPrompt.prompt
documentation: The prompt to be used for warm handoff. Can contain dynamic variables.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallToolWarmTransferOptionWarmTransferStaticMessage.message
documentation: The static message to be used for warm handoff. Can contain dynamic variables.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state edges).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallTool.number
documentation:
The number to transfer to in E.164 format or a dynamic variable like
{{transfer_number}}.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallTool.description
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallTool.show_transferee_as_caller
documentation:
If set to true, will show transferee (the user, not the AI agent) as caller when
transferring, requires the telephony side to support SIP REFER to PSTN. This is
only applicable for cold transfer, so if warm transfer option is specified, this
field will be ignored. Default to false (default to show AI agent as caller).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolTransferCallTool.warm_transfer_option
documentation:
If set, when transfer is successful, will perform a warm handoff.

Can leave either a static message or a dynamic one based on prompt. Set to null
to disable warm handoff.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCheckAvailabilityCalTool.cal_api_key
documentation:
Cal.com Api key that have access to the cal.com event you want to check
availability for.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCheckAvailabilityCalTool.event_type_id
documentation:
Cal.com event type id number for the cal.com event you want to check
availability for.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCheckAvailabilityCalTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state transitions). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCheckAvailabilityCalTool.description
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCheckAvailabilityCalTool.timezone
documentation:
Timezone to be used when checking availability, must be in
[IANA timezone database](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones).
If not specified, will check if user specified timezone in call, and if not,
will use the timezone of the Retell servers.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolBookAppointmentCalTool.cal_api_key
documentation:
Cal.com Api key that have access to the cal.com event you want to book
appointment.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolBookAppointmentCalTool.event_type_id
documentation: Cal.com event type id number for the cal.com event you want to book appointment.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolBookAppointmentCalTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state transitions). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolBookAppointmentCalTool.description
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolBookAppointmentCalTool.timezone
documentation:
Timezone to be used when booking appointment, must be in
[IANA timezone database](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones).
If not specified, will check if user specified timezone in call, and if not,
will use the timezone of the Retell servers.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolPressDigitTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state transitions). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolPressDigitTool.delay_ms
documentation:
Delay in milliseconds before pressing the digit, because a lot of IVR systems
speak very slowly, and a delay can make sure the agent hears the full menu.
Default to 1000 ms (1s). Valid range is 0 to 5000 ms (inclusive).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolPressDigitTool.description
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomToolParameters.properties
documentation:
The value of properties is an object, where each key is the name of a property
and each value is a schema used to validate that property.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomToolParameters.type
documentation: Type must be "object" for a JSON Schema object.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomToolParameters.required
documentation:
List of names of required property when generating this parameter.

LLM will do its best to generate the required properties in its function
arguments. Property must exist in properties.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.description
documentation: Describes what this tool does and when to call this tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state edges). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.speak_after_execution
documentation:
Determines whether the agent would call LLM another time and speak when the
result of function is obtained. Usually this needs to get turned on so user can
get update for the function call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.speak_during_execution
documentation:
Determines whether the agent would say sentence like "One moment, let me check
that." when executing the function. Recommend to turn on if your function call
takes over 1s (including network) to complete, so that your agent remains
responsive.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.url
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.execution_message_description
documentation:
The description for the sentence agent say during execution.

Only applicable when speak_during_execution is true. Can write what to say or
even provide examples. The default is "The message you will say to callee when
calling this tool. Make sure it fits into the conversation smoothly.".

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.parameters
documentation:
The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.

See [JSON Schema reference](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/)
for documentation about the format. Omitting parameters defines a function with
an empty parameter list.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.GeneralToolCustomTool.timeout_ms
documentation:
The maximum time in milliseconds the tool can run before it's considered
timeout.

If the tool times out, the agent would have that info. The minimum value allowed
is 1000 ms (1 s), and maximum value allowed is 600,000 ms (10 min). By default,
this is set to 120,000 ms (2 min).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.StateEdgeParameters.properties
documentation:
The value of properties is an object, where each key is the name of a property
and each value is a schema used to validate that property.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.StateEdgeParameters.type
documentation: Type must be "object" for a JSON Schema object.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.StateEdgeParameters.required
documentation:
List of names of required property when generating this parameter.

LLM will do its best to generate the required properties in its function
arguments. Property must exist in properties.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.StateEdge.description
documentation:
Describes what's the transition and at what time / criteria should this
transition happen.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.StateEdge.destination_state_name
documentation:
The destination state name when going through transition of state via this edge.

State transition internally is implemented as a tool call of LLM, and a tool
call with name "transition*to*{destination_state_name}" will get created. Feel
free to reference it inside the prompt.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_update_params.StateEdge.parameters
documentation:
Describes what parameters you want to extract out when the transition changes.

The parameters extracted here can be referenced in prompts & function

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.LlmCreateParams.model_temperature
documentation:

value means more random. If unset, default value 0 will apply. Note that for
tool calling, a lower value is recommended.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.LlmCreateParams.s2s_model

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.LlmCreateParams.starting_state

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.LlmCreateParams.states
documentation:
States of the LLM.

This is to help reduce prompt length and tool choices when the call can be
broken into distinct states. With shorter prompts and less tools, the LLM can
better focus and follow the rules, minimizing hallucination. If this field is
not set, the agent would only have general prompt and general tools (essentially
one state).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.LlmCreateParams.tool_call_strict_mode
documentation:

follow the json schema. The time to save a new tool or change to a tool will be
longer as additional processing is needed. Default to false.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolBookAppointmentCalTool.timezone
documentation:
Timezone to be used when booking appointment, must be in
[IANA timezone database](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones).
If not specified, will check if user specified timezone in call, and if not,
will use the timezone of the Retell servers.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolPressDigitTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state transitions). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolPressDigitTool.delay_ms
documentation:
Delay in milliseconds before pressing the digit, because a lot of IVR systems
speak very slowly, and a delay can make sure the agent hears the full menu.
Default to 1000 ms (1s). Valid range is 0 to 5000 ms (inclusive).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolPressDigitTool.description
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomToolParameters.properties
documentation:
The value of properties is an object, where each key is the name of a property
and each value is a schema used to validate that property.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomToolParameters.type
documentation: Type must be "object" for a JSON Schema object.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomToolParameters.required
documentation:
List of names of required property when generating this parameter.

LLM will do its best to generate the required properties in its function
arguments. Property must exist in properties.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.description
documentation: Describes what this tool does and when to call this tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.name
documentation:
Name of the tool.

Must be unique within all tools available to LLM at any given time (general
tools + state tools + state edges). Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or
contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.speak_after_execution
documentation:
Determines whether the agent would call LLM another time and speak when the
result of function is obtained. Usually this needs to get turned on so user can
get update for the function call.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.speak_during_execution
documentation:
Determines whether the agent would say sentence like "One moment, let me check
that." when executing the function. Recommend to turn on if your function call
takes over 1s (including network) to complete, so that your agent remains
responsive.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.url
documentation:
Describes what the tool does, sometimes can also include information about when
to call the tool.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.execution_message_description
documentation:
The description for the sentence agent say during execution.

Only applicable when speak_during_execution is true. Can write what to say or
even provide examples. The default is "The message you will say to callee when
calling this tool. Make sure it fits into the conversation smoothly.".

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.parameters
documentation:
The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.

See [JSON Schema reference](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/)
for documentation about the format. Omitting parameters defines a function with
an empty parameter list.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.StateToolCustomTool.timeout_ms
documentation:
The maximum time in milliseconds the tool can run before it's considered
timeout.

If the tool times out, the agent would have that info. The minimum value allowed
is 1000 ms (1 s), and maximum value allowed is 600,000 ms (10 min). By default,
this is set to 120,000 ms (2 min).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.State.name
documentation:
Name of the state, must be unique for each state.

Must be consisted of a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a
maximum length of 64 (no space allowed).

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.State.edges
documentation: Edges of the state define how and what state can be reached from this state.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.State.state_prompt
documentation:
Prompt of the state, will be appended to the system prompt of LLM.

- System prompt = general prompt + state prompt.

class_variables.name: retell.types.llm_create_params.State.tools
documentation:
A list of tools specific to this state the model may call (to get external
knowledge, call API, etc). You can select from some common predefined tools like
end call, transfer call, etc; or you can create your own custom tool (last
option) for the LLM to use.

- Tools of LLM = general tools + state tools + state transitions

methods.name: retell.resources.llm.LlmResource.create
documentation:
Create a new Retell LLM

Args:
  begin_message: First utterance said by the agent in the call. If not set, LLM will dynamically
      generate a message. If set to "", agent will wait for user to speak first.

  general_prompt: General prompt appended to system prompt no matter what state the agent is in.

      - System prompt (with state) = general prompt + state prompt.

      - System prompt (no state) = general prompt.

  general_tools: A list of tools the model may call (to get external knowledge, call API, etc).
      You can select from some common predefined tools like end call, transfer call,
      etc; or you can create your own custom tool (last option) for the LLM to use.

      - Tools of LLM (with state) = general tools + state tools + state transitions

      - Tools of LLM (no state) = general tools

  inbound_dynamic_variables_webhook_url: For inbound phone calls, if this webhook is set, will POST to it to retrieve
      dynamic variables to use for the call. Without this, there's no way to pass
      dynamic variables for inbound calls.

  knowledge_base_ids: A list of knowledge base ids to use for this resource. Set to null to remove all
      knowledge bases.

  model: Select the underlying text LLM. If not set, would default to gpt-4o.

  model_temperature: If set, will control the randomness of the response. Value ranging from [0,1].
      Lower value means more deterministic, while higher value means more random. If
      unset, default value 0 will apply. Note that for tool calling, a lower value is
      recommended.

  s2s_model: Select the underlying speech to speech model. Can only set this or model, not
      both.

  starting_state: Name of the starting state. Required if states is not empty.

  states: States of the LLM. This is to help reduce prompt length and tool choices when
      the call can be broken into distinct states. With shorter prompts and less
      tools, the LLM can better focus and follow the rules, minimizing hallucination.
      If this field is not set, the agent would only have general prompt and general
      tools (essentially one state).

  tool_call_strict_mode: Only applicable when model is gpt-4o or gpt-4o mini. If set to true, will use
      structured output to make sure tool call arguments follow the json schema. The
      time to save a new tool or change to a tool will be longer as additional
      processing is needed. Default to false.

  extra_headers: Send extra headers

  extra_query: Add additional query parameters to the request

  extra_body: Add additional JSON properties to the request

  timeout: Override the client-level default timeout for this request, in seconds

For lower-level control, see `.read()`, `.json()`, `.iter_bytes()`.

You can customise the type that the response is parsed into through
the `to` argument, e.g.

```py
from retell import BaseModel


class MyModel(BaseModel):
    foo: str


obj = response.parse(to=MyModel)
print(obj.foo)
```

  - `list`
  - `Union`
  - `str`
  - `int`
  - `float`
